name: Strait of Hormuz
notes: ~21 mb/d crude+products observed; physical limit well above

name: Bab el-Mandeb
notes: ~9 mb/d historically; dropped to ~4 in 2024 due to Houthi attacks

name: Suez Canal + SUMED pipeline
notes: Suez ~5-6 mb/d + SUMED ~2.5 mb/d; post-2023 Red Sea disruption reduced usage

name: Turkish Straits (Bosphorus + Dardanelles)
notes: ~3 mb/d of Russian and Caspian crude

name: Danish Straits
notes: Russian Baltic exports; collapsed post-2022 sanctions

name: Strait of Malacca
notes: ~16 mb/d crude+condensate; narrowest 1.7 nm at Phillips Channel

name: Panama Canal
notes: Tankers limited; 2023-2024 drought cut daily slots to 22-24

name: Gulf of Aden to Indian Ocean (open sea)
Gibraltar to North Atlantic
notes: Gibraltar strait is wide; not capacity-constrained for oil

name: Cape of Good Hope
notes: Long alternative to Suez; became heavily used in 2024

name: South China Sea to Western Pacific
notes: Taiwan / Luzon straits; no chokepoint for oil
